posts.raw_content:
Absolutely no one that I encounter in real life or off-line actually knows what they are talking about when they throw around the words 'Socialism' and 'Communism' in modern discourse.

According to classical Marxist theory, Communism is the theoretical, utopian end-state that is supposedly achieved through the forced implementation of a Socialist transition government. By Marxism's own definition, a true Communist society has never actually existed.

The only difference between the two is that Socialism is a reality that kills people, while Communism is the fairy tale used to justify it.

It is no accident that it was called the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, not the Union of Soviet Communist Republics.

To be fair, that is just how socialism is defined within Marxism. Outside of Marxist circles during the first half of the 20th century, there were hundreds of completely different, competing definitions all existing at the exact same time. But when Bernie Sanders or Mamdani tell us they are "Democratic Socialists" they are clearly operating within a Marxist framework; and letting us know that they intend to use the democratic process to usher in a Socialist death-state.

Why do politicians keep screwing this up? Because they aren't using definitions, they're using marketing. The Right uses 'Socialism' as a lazy buzzword for any spending they dislike, while the Left uses 'Democratic Socialism' to make it sound like "Socialism Lite" to cover up their radical Marxist goals. It reduces a deadly historical reality to a cheap campaign buzzword, keeping the public completely blind to what is actually being ushered in.
